question.text: Employer's contribution to NPS under Section 80CCD(2) is allowed as a deduction up to:

answer.text: 10% of basic salary + DA (14% for central govt and in new regime)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 80CCD(2), the employer's contribution to NPS is deductible up to 10% of basic salary and dearness allowance (14% for central government employees and under new tax regime). This is ABOVE the 80C limit. **The Trap:** 12% is the EPF contribution rate, not the NPS employer deduction limit. Rs.1,50,000 is the 80C limit. 80CCD(2) is separate from and additional to 80C. **Cross-Exam Context:** Employer NPS contribution tax treatment is tested in Series XVII and X-A. This is one of the most valuable tax benefits of NPS. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 80CCD(2) is the most attractive NPS tax benefit because it is ABOVE the 80C limit with no separate cap. Encourage corporate employees to request employer NPS contributions.
